perturbation.original_text: All the information that (i) i s written and marked as CONFID ENTIAL, or (ii) is disclosed verbally, and at the time of its disclosure, the Disclosing Party's identify such information to be protected in accordance with t his Agreement ( "Confidential Information" ), shall be considered confidenti al and restricted property of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.changed_text: All the information that (i) i s written and marked as CONFID ENTIAL, or (ii) is disclosed verbally, even if the Disclosing Party does not explicitly identify such information as protected at the time of disclosure, shall be considered confidential and restricted property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that verbally disclosed information must be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure. The changed text removes this requirement, stating that all verbally disclosed information is confidential regardless of explicit identification. This contradicts the original definition of 'Confidential Information', potentially encompassing even inadvertently shared details. This creates ambiguity over what constitutes confidential information. This change introduces a contradiction with section 7, where the definition of what is 'not confidential' is listed. If any information is disclosed and not marked as confidential, but is being treated as such, than in text is contradicting section 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall not include info rmation which is: a. The information was already known by the Receiving Party prior to its disclosure by a legal and legitimate way, not subject to any obligation to be kept confid ential; b. There has been prior written consent of Disclosing Party , to release the obligation of secrecy and confidentiality; c. Lawfully obtained by the Receiving Party from a third party without restrictions as to use and disclose ; d. If the Receiving Party receives a court order to disclose Confidential Information . In this case Receiving Party shall (i) promptly notify the Disclosing Party in order to allow the latter to act and avoid such disclosure, and (ii) upon request, but provided that it complies with applicable Law, cooperate with the Disclosing Party in preventing such a disclosure; e. Publicly available through lawful disclosure; or f. Independently developed by or for the Receiving Party .
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include verbally disclosed information even without explicit identification as such at the time of disclosure. This directly contradicts section 7, which lists exceptions to what constitutes 'Confidential Information', such as information already known by the Receiving Party or lawfully obtained from a third party. If verbally disclosed information is automatically confidential regardless of explicit marking, it conflicts with section 7's exceptions. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document, as information not explicitly marked as confidential but verbally disclosed would be considered confidential under the changed text, contradicting the explicit exemptions in section 7.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees for the period specified in Section 13 that it will: a. Restrict disclosure of the Confidential Information to the minimum required number of people (such as employees, directors, office rs, legal counsels, accountants or consultants who shall also be personally bound to maintain the Information in confidence); b. Direct its employees, agents and subcontractors to abide by non-disclosure terms at least as protective of the Confidential Informati on as those set forth herein; c. Maintain the confidentiality a nd take proper measures to pro tect the secrecy of the Confidential Information , using the same degree of care t hat it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in any event shall us e at least commercially reasonable care .; d. Immediately notify the other party of any misappropriation o r misuse of Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees for the period specified in Section 13 that it will: a. Restrict disclosure of the Confidential Information to individuals whom it deems necessary, regardless of their pre-existing confidentiality obligations; b. Ensure its employees, agents, and subcontractors are aware of the confidential nature of the information, but are not required to sign additional non-disclosure agreements; c. Take reasonable steps to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information; d. Notify the Disclosing Party of any misappropriation or misuse of Confidential Information only if it results in significant financial loss.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires restricting disclosure to the minimum necessary number of people who are bound by confidentiality, directing employees/agents to abide by non-disclosure terms, using the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own confidential information, and immediately notifying the other party of any misappropriation or misuse of confidential information. The modified text introduces several contradictions. Subclause a. allows disclosure to anyone deemed 'necessary', irrespective of confidentiality obligations, contradicting the need for confidentiality. Subclause b. changes the requirement for employees/agents to abide by non-disclosure terms to merely being 'aware', weakening the protection. Subclause c. now it just says take reasonable steps, removing any specification on care. Subclause d. only notifies if it results in significant loss. This is contradicting the original intent that it should be immeditately regardless of the size. The changes together weaken the confidentiality obligations and create uncertainty about the Receiving Party's duties, contradicting with section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees for the period specified in Section 13 that it will: a. Restrict disclosure of the Confidential Information to the minimum required number of people (such as employees, directors, office rs, legal counsels, accountants or consultants who shall also be personally bound to maintain the Information in confidence); b. Direct its employees, agents and subcontractors to abide by non-disclosure terms at least as protective of the Confidential Informati on as those set forth herein; c. Maintain the confidentiality a nd take proper measures to pro tect the secrecy of the Confidential Information , using the same degree of care t hat it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in any event shall us e at least commercially reasonable care .; d. Immediately notify the other party of any misappropriation o r misuse of Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction with the contradicted text. Specifically, the modified text weakens the confidentiality obligations in several ways: a. allows disclosure to anyone deemed 'necessary', irrespective of confidentiality obligations, contradicting the need for confidentiality stated in the contradicted text. b. changes the requirement for employees/agents to abide by non-disclosure terms to merely being 'aware', weakening the protection stated in the contradicted text. c. now it just says take reasonable steps, removing any specification on care, which contradicts the specific care outlined in the contradicted text. d. only notifies if it results in significant loss, contradicting the requirement for immediate notification in the contradicted text. These changes create direct conflicts regarding the scope and enforcement of confidentiality obligations, leading to a strong in-text contradiction, especially as the Change Text and Contradicted text are in the same section.

perturbation.original_text: The breach of the terms s et forth herein imply: 2 de 2 PromonLogicalis Non-Disclosure Agreement (version August 2015) a. Termination of any agreement executed by and between the Par ties, without any obligation to compensation for the complainant, being due, however, the penal ties for breach of contract by the party which gives cause to termination; b. Payment of damages as set fort h in Section 9 and the applica ble law.
perturbation.changed_text: The breach of the terms s et forth herein may, at the Disclosing Party's sole discretion, imply: 2 de 2 PromonLogicalis Non-Disclosure Agreement (version August 2015) a. Termination of any agreement executed by and between the Par ties, potentially requiring compensation for the complainant, depending on the severity of the breach; b. Payment of damages as determined by a mutually agreed-upon arbitration process.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that a breach of the agreement *implies* automatic termination without compensation for the complainant. The revised version states that a breach *may*, at the Disclosing Party's *sole discretion*, imply termination, and introduces the possibility of compensation depending on the 'severity' and goes through a mutually agreed-upon arbitration process. This creates in-text contradiction by changing the outcome to not be automatic and it is at the discretion of one of the parties. Section 9 discusses damages, it should'nt have to go through arbitration to determine payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge that the breach of secrecy has the c haracter of serious irregularity and can cause serious damages to the other Party. 9.1 In case of breach of any obligation set forth herein by th e Receiving Party , the Disclosing Party shall recover any and all damages. Notwithstanding any other provisio n herein, neither Party shall be liable for indirect damages o r loss of profits. 9.2 Accordingly, the actual or t hreatened unauthorized disclosu re or use of any Information shall give the Disclosing Party the right to seek injunctive relief restraining such unauthoriz ed disclosure or use, without the necessity of proving actual damages, in addition to any other remedy otherwise available to the Disclosing Party . Any third party owner of Information disclosed by a party he reto shall be an intended third party beneficiary of this Agreement and shall be entitled to rely upon and directly enforce the terms and conditions hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text states a breach *implies* automatic termination without compensation, while the changed text states the breach *may* imply termination, at the Disclosing Party's sole discretion, and introduces potential compensation depending on the breach severity and determined by arbitration. This change contradicts the Contradicted Text which states that in case of breach the Disclosing Party *shall* recover damages and has the right to seek injunctive relief without proving actual damages. The changed text makes the outcome discretionary and introduces arbitration, conflicting with the mandatory recovery and injunctive relief rights outlined in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: All information (either Confidential Information or not) fur nished under this Agreement shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall be returned to it or destroyed promptly at its reque st (together with all relevant copies, extracts, plan s, schematics or other reproduct ions).
perturbation.changed_text: All information (either Confidential Information or not) furnished under this Agreement may, at the Receiving Party's discretion, be retained for archival purposes, even after the termination of the agreement. The Receiving Party is only required to return or destroy Confidential Information containing trade secrets. Other information may be retained.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause stipulates that *all* information furnished under the agreement must be returned or destroyed promptly upon request. The modified version introduces an exception, allowing the Receiving Party to retain information for archival purposes, except for Confidential Information containing trade secrets. This creates an in-text contradiction, as it is now ambiguous whether the Receiving Party must return or destroy all information, or if they can retain some information for archival purposes. This contradicts the obligation of section 4 to keep the information only for the purpose of this agreement and is contradicting section 10, as it allowing the receiving party to use the information for its own benefit.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither the execution of this Agreement nor the furnishing of any information under this Agreement shall be construed as granting any Party or any of its representatives, either expressly or by implicat ion, any license or right to use any Confidential Information for its own benefit or the benef it of any other person, firm o r entity, and each party expressly agrees not to so use any such information. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as conferring any rights, by license or oth erwise, to any invention, discovery, or improvement made, conceived or acquired prior to, during or after the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that all information, confidential or not, must be returned or destroyed upon request. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing the Receiving Party to retain information (excluding trade secrets) for archival purposes even after the agreement's termination. This directly contradicts the provided "Contradicted Text," which states that no party has the right to use any Confidential Information for its own benefit and expressly agrees not to do so, as well as not construing any rights by license to use any invention or discovery. The changed text allows retention, which can be construed as use for their own benefit, therefore violating the terms set in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement binds not onl y the parties, but also their s uccessors and assignees.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement binds only the parties and their direct employees who have signed an acknowledgement of this agreement. It does not extend to successors, assignees, or any external third parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the agreement binds successors and assignees. The modified version restricts the binding to the parties and specific employees, excluding successors, assignees, and external parties. This contradicts the original scope and creates uncertainty about who is bound by the agreement, especially in scenarios such as mergers or acquisitions and is contradicting Section 5 where it specifies the people who are bound to mainting confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees for the period specified in Section 13 that it will: a. Restrict disclosure of the Confidential Information to the minimum required number of people (such as employees, directors, office rs, legal counsels, accountants or consultants who shall also be personally bound to maintain the Information in confidence); b. Direct its employees, agents and subcontractors to abide by non-disclosure terms at least as protective of the Confidential Informati on as those set forth herein; c. Maintain the confidentiality a nd take proper measures to pro tect the secrecy of the Confidential Information , using the same degree of care t hat it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in any event shall us e at least commercially reasonable care .; d. Immediately notify the other party of any misappropriation o r misuse of Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement binds successors and assignees. The changed text restricts the binding to only the parties and their direct employees, explicitly excluding successors and assignees. The contradicted text (Section 5) requires the Receiving Party to ensure that certain individuals (employees, agents, subcontractors) abide by non-disclosure terms. The change directly contradicts Section 5 because if successors and assignees are no longer bound by the agreement, they are not obligated to maintain confidentiality, potentially violating the terms outlined in Section 5.
